_coverage: partial: [--help, --version, --dump-user-agent, --list-extractors, --extractor-descriptions, --ap-list-mso, --rm-cache-dir skipped (meta/info flags that print and exit); --no-* negation flags omitted where the positive is the default; many niche debugging flags omitted for GUI usability]
description: Feature-rich command-line audio/video downloader. Fork of youtube-dl with additional features and active maintenance. Supports thousands of sites.

arguments.name: Format
arguments.description: Video format code or selector expression. Use -F to list available formats. Supports complex expressions like 'bestvideo[height<=720]+bestaudio/best[height<=720]'. Use 'ba' for best audio, 'bv' for best video.

arguments.name: Format Sort
arguments.description: Sort the formats by the fields given. Comma-separated list of fields. Overrides default format sorting.

arguments.name: List Formats
arguments.description: List all available formats of each video. Simulates unless --no-simulate is used.

arguments.name: Merge Output Format
arguments.description: Container format to use when merging separate video and audio streams. Only applies when a merge is required (e.g. bestvideo+bestaudio).

arguments.name: Prefer Free Formats
arguments.description: Prefer video formats with free containers (webm, ogg) over non-free ones (mp4) of same quality. Use with '-S ext' to strictly prefer free containers.

arguments.name: Check Formats
arguments.description: Make sure formats are selected only from those that are actually downloadable.

arguments.name: Video Multistreams
arguments.description: Allow multiple video streams to be merged into a single file.

arguments.name: Audio Multistreams
arguments.description: Allow multiple audio streams to be merged into a single file.

arguments.name: Output Template
arguments.description: Output filename template. Uses Python-style format strings with video metadata fields. See yt-dlp docs for full field reference.

arguments.name: Paths
arguments.description: Directory where files should be downloaded. Can specify type-specific paths with TYPE:PATH syntax (e.g. 'home:/downloads' or 'temp:/tmp').

arguments.name: Output NA Placeholder
arguments.description: Placeholder value for unavailable metadata fields in the output filename template.

arguments.name: Restrict Filenames
arguments.description: Restrict filenames to only ASCII characters, and avoid '&' and spaces in filenames. Useful for compatibility with older systems or scripts.

arguments.name: Windows Filenames
arguments.description: Force filenames to be Windows-compatible regardless of current OS.

arguments.name: Trim Filenames
arguments.description: Limit the filename length (excluding extension) to the specified number of characters.

arguments.name: No Overwrites
arguments.description: Do not overwrite any files.

arguments.name: Force Overwrites
arguments.description: Overwrite all video and metadata files. This option includes --no-continue.

arguments.name: Continue
arguments.description: Resume partially downloaded files/fragments. This is the default behavior.

arguments.name: No Part Files
arguments.description: Do not use .part files — write directly into the output file. Risk of corruption if download is interrupted.

arguments.name: No Mtime
arguments.description: Do not use the Last-modified header to set the file modification time. Sets modification time to the download time instead.

arguments.name: Playlist Start
arguments.description: Playlist video to start at. 1 is the first video.

arguments.name: Playlist End
arguments.description: Playlist video to end at. Downloads from start through this index.

arguments.name: Playlist Items
arguments.description: Specific playlist video indices to download. Supports comma-separated indices and ranges.

arguments.name: Min Filesize
arguments.description: Do not download any videos smaller than this size.

arguments.name: Max Filesize
arguments.description: Do not download any videos larger than this size.

arguments.name: Date
arguments.description: Download only videos uploaded on this date. Format: YYYYMMDD or relative like 'today-1week'.

arguments.name: Date Before
arguments.description: Download only videos uploaded on or before this date. Same format as --date.

arguments.name: Date After
arguments.description: Download only videos uploaded on or after this date. Same format as --date.

arguments.name: Match Filters
arguments.description: Generic video filter. Any metadata field can be compared using operators. Use '!' prefix to check field is not present. Multiple filters are OR-combined.

arguments.name: Download Only Video
arguments.description: Download only the video, if the URL refers to a video and a playlist.

arguments.name: Download Full Playlist
arguments.description: Download the playlist, if the URL refers to a video and a playlist.

arguments.name: Age Limit
arguments.description: Download only videos suitable for the given age.

arguments.name: Download Archive
arguments.description: Download only videos not listed in the archive file. Record the IDs of all downloaded videos in it. Useful for incremental playlist downloads.

arguments.name: Max Downloads
arguments.description: Abort after downloading this many files.

arguments.name: Break On Existing
arguments.description: Stop the download process when encountering a file that is already in the archive.

arguments.name: Break On Reject
arguments.description: Stop the download process when encountering a file that has been filtered out.

arguments.name: Skip Playlist After Errors
arguments.description: Number of allowed download failures until the rest of the playlist is skipped.

arguments.name: Concurrent Fragments
arguments.description: Number of fragments of a DASH/HLS video that should be downloaded concurrently. Higher values speed up fragmented downloads but use more bandwidth.

arguments.name: Rate Limit
arguments.description: Maximum download rate in bytes per second. Supports K, M suffixes.

arguments.name: Throttled Rate
arguments.description: Minimum download rate below which throttling is assumed and the video data is re-extracted. Useful for bypassing YouTube throttling.

arguments.name: Retries
arguments.description: Number of retries for download errors. Use 'infinite' for unlimited retries.

arguments.name: Fragment Retries
arguments.description: Number of retries for a fragment (DASH, HLS, ISM). Use 'infinite' for unlimited.

arguments.name: Buffer Size
arguments.description: Size of download buffer.

arguments.name: HTTP Chunk Size
arguments.description: Size of a chunk for chunk-based HTTP downloading. May help bypass bandwidth throttling (experimental).

arguments.name: Keep Fragments
arguments.description: Keep downloaded fragments on disk after downloading is finished. By default they are deleted.

arguments.name: Playlist Reverse
arguments.description: Download playlist videos in reverse order.

arguments.name: Playlist Random
arguments.description: Download playlist videos in random order.

arguments.name: HLS Use MPEGTS
arguments.description: Use the mpegts container for HLS videos. Allows some players to play while downloading and reduces corruption risk on interruption. Enabled by default for live streams.

arguments.name: External Downloader
arguments.description: Name or path of external downloader to use. Can be protocol-prefixed (e.g. 'dash,m3u8:native'). Recommended: aria2c for faster multi-connection downloads.

arguments.name: External Downloader Args
arguments.description: Arguments for the external downloader. Format: NAME:ARGS. Can be specified multiple times for different downloaders.

arguments.name: Extract Audio
arguments.description: Convert video files to audio-only files. Requires ffmpeg and ffprobe.

arguments.name: Audio Format
arguments.description: Audio format to convert to when -x/--extract-audio is used.

arguments.name: Audio Quality
arguments.description: Audio quality when converting with -x. Value between 0 (best) and 10 (worst) for VBR, or a specific bitrate like 128K.

arguments.name: Remux Video
arguments.description: Remux the video into another container if necessary. Supports rule-based syntax for multiple remappings.

arguments.name: Recode Video
arguments.description: Re-encode the video into another format if re-encoding is necessary. Same syntax as --remux-video.

arguments.name: Keep Video
arguments.description: Keep the intermediate video file on disk after post-processing. By default it is deleted.

arguments.name: Embed Subtitles
arguments.description: Embed subtitles in the video. Only works for mp4, webm, and mkv containers.

arguments.name: Embed Thumbnail
arguments.description: Embed thumbnail in the video as cover art.

arguments.name: Embed Metadata
arguments.description: Embed metadata to the video file. Also embeds chapters and infojson if present unless explicitly disabled.

arguments.name: Embed Chapters
arguments.description: Add chapter markers to the video file.

arguments.name: Embed Info JSON
arguments.description: Embed the infojson as an attachment to mkv/mka video files.

arguments.name: Convert Subtitles
arguments.description: Convert the subtitles to another format.

arguments.name: Convert Thumbnails
arguments.description: Convert the thumbnails to another format.

arguments.name: Split Chapters
arguments.description: Split video into multiple files based on internal chapters. Use the 'chapter:' prefix with --paths and --output to set split file names.

arguments.name: Remove Chapters
arguments.description: Remove chapters whose title matches the given regular expression. Time ranges prefixed by '*' can also be used. Can be specified multiple times.

arguments.name: Force Keyframes At Cuts
arguments.description: Force keyframes around the chapters before removing/splitting them. Requires a re-encode and is very slow, but reduces artifacts around cuts.

arguments.name: Fixup
arguments.description: Automatically correct known faults of the file.

arguments.name: FFmpeg Location
arguments.description: Location of the ffmpeg binary; either the path to the binary or its containing directory.

arguments.name: Postprocessor Args
arguments.description: Arguments for postprocessors. Format: NAME:ARGS. Supports PP+EXE:ARGS syntax. Use for passing custom ffmpeg arguments.

arguments.name: Exec Command
arguments.description: Execute a command after download. Supports output template syntax for field substitution. %(filepath)q is appended if no fields are passed.

arguments.name: Write Subtitles
arguments.description: Write subtitle file to disk.

arguments.name: Write Auto Subtitles
arguments.description: Write automatically generated subtitle file (e.g. YouTube auto-captions).

arguments.name: List Subtitles
arguments.description: List available subtitles of each video. Simulates unless --no-simulate is used.

arguments.name: Subtitle Format
arguments.description: Subtitle format preference. Accepts format preference separated by '/'.

arguments.name: Subtitle Languages
arguments.description: Languages of the subtitles to download. Comma-separated, supports regex. Prefix with '-' to exclude.

arguments.name: Write Thumbnail
arguments.description: Write thumbnail image to disk.

arguments.name: Write All Thumbnails
arguments.description: Write all thumbnail image formats to disk.

arguments.name: List Thumbnails
arguments.description: List available thumbnails of each video. Simulates unless --no-simulate is used.

arguments.name: Write Description
arguments.description: Write video description to a .description file.

arguments.name: Write Info JSON
arguments.description: Write video metadata to a .info.json file. May contain personal information.

arguments.name: Clean Info JSON
arguments.description: Remove some private fields such as filenames from the infojson. Enabled by default.

arguments.name: Write Comments
arguments.description: Retrieve video comments to be placed in the infojson.

arguments.name: Parse Metadata
arguments.description: Parse additional metadata like title/artist from other fields. Format: FROM:TO using Python regex named groups.

arguments.name: Replace In Metadata
arguments.description: Replace text in a metadata field using regex. Format: FIELDS REGEX REPLACE.

arguments.name: XAttrs
arguments.description: Write metadata to the video file's xattrs using Dublin Core and XDG standards.

arguments.name: Username
arguments.description: Login with this account ID.

arguments.name: Password
arguments.description: Account password. If omitted, yt-dlp will ask interactively.

arguments.name: Two-Factor Code
arguments.description: Two-factor authentication code.

arguments.name: Use Netrc
arguments.description: Use .netrc authentication data.

arguments.name: Netrc Location
arguments.description: Location of .netrc authentication data; either the path or its containing directory.

arguments.name: Video Password
arguments.description: Video-specific password (vimeo, youku).

arguments.name: Cookies File
arguments.description: Netscape-formatted file to read cookies from and dump cookie jar in. Use for sites requiring login.

arguments.name: Cookies From Browser
arguments.description: Browser and optional profile to load cookies from. Format: BROWSER[+KEYRING][:PROFILE]. Supported: brave, chrome, chromium, edge, firefox, opera, safari, vivaldi.

arguments.name: Proxy
arguments.description: HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS proxy URL. Pass empty string for direct connection.

arguments.name: Socket Timeout
arguments.description: Time to wait before giving up, in seconds.

arguments.name: Source Address
arguments.description: Client-side IP address to bind to.

arguments.name: Force IPv4
arguments.description: Make all connections via IPv4.

arguments.name: Force IPv6
arguments.description: Make all connections via IPv6.

arguments.name: Geo Verification Proxy
arguments.description: Proxy used to verify the IP address for some geo-restricted sites. The main --proxy is used for actual downloading.

arguments.name: Geo Bypass
arguments.description: Bypass geographic restriction via faking X-Forwarded-For HTTP header. Enabled by default.

arguments.name: Geo Bypass Country
arguments.description: Force bypass geographic restriction with a specific two-letter ISO 3166-2 country code.

arguments.name: Geo Bypass IP Block
arguments.description: Force bypass geographic restriction with an explicitly provided IP block in CIDR notation.

arguments.name: Quiet
arguments.description: Activate quiet mode. If used with --verbose, print the log to stderr.

arguments.name: No Warnings
arguments.description: Ignore warnings.

arguments.name: Simulate
arguments.description: Do not download the video and do not write anything to disk.

arguments.name: Skip Download
arguments.description: Do not download the video but write all related files (subtitles, thumbnails, metadata, etc).

arguments.name: Dump JSON
arguments.description: Quiet, but print JSON information for each video. Simulates unless --no-simulate is used.

arguments.name: Dump Single JSON
arguments.description: Quiet, but print JSON information for each URL. For playlists, the whole playlist information is dumped in a single line.

arguments.name: Print Field
arguments.description: Field name or output template to print to screen. Can be prefixed with WHEN: to control timing. Implies --quiet and --simulate.

arguments.name: Verbose
arguments.description: Print various debugging information.

arguments.name: Newline
arguments.description: Output progress bar as new lines instead of updating in place.

arguments.name: No Progress
arguments.description: Do not print progress bar.

arguments.name: Console Title
arguments.description: Display progress in console titlebar.

arguments.name: Print To File
arguments.description: Append given template to a file. Format: [WHEN:]TEMPLATE FILE.

arguments.name: Force Write Archive
arguments.description: Force download archive entries to be written even if --simulate is used.

arguments.name: Ignore Errors
arguments.description: Ignore download and postprocessing errors. The download will be considered successful even if postprocessing fails.

arguments.name: Abort On Error
arguments.description: Abort downloading of further videos if an error occurs.

arguments.name: Force Generic Extractor
arguments.description: Force extraction to use the generic extractor instead of site-specific ones.

arguments.name: Default Search
arguments.description: Prefix for unqualified URLs. Use 'auto' to let yt-dlp guess, 'error' to throw an error.

arguments.name: Flat Playlist
arguments.description: Do not extract the videos of a playlist, only list them.

arguments.name: Live From Start
arguments.description: Download livestreams from the start. Currently only supported for YouTube (experimental).

arguments.name: Wait For Video
arguments.description: Wait for scheduled streams to become available. Pass minimum seconds (or range MIN-MAX) to wait between retries.

arguments.name: Mark Watched
arguments.description: Mark videos watched (even with --simulate).

arguments.name: No Colors
arguments.description: Do not emit color codes in output. Useful when piping output.

arguments.name: Compat Options
arguments.description: Options to revert some yt-dlp changes for compatibility with youtube-dl or youtube-dlc configurations.

arguments.name: Batch File
arguments.description: File containing URLs to download, one URL per line. Use '-' for stdin. Lines starting with '#', ';' or ']' are comments.

arguments.name: Concat Playlist
arguments.description: Concatenate videos in a playlist. All files must have same codecs and number of streams.

arguments.name: No Check Certificates
arguments.description: Suppress HTTPS certificate validation. INSECURE: only use if you understand the risks.

arguments.name: Prefer Insecure
arguments.description: Use an unencrypted connection to retrieve video info. Currently only supported for YouTube.

arguments.name: Legacy Server Connect
arguments.description: Explicitly allow HTTPS connection to servers that do not support RFC 5746 secure renegotiation.

arguments.name: Add Header
arguments.description: Specify a custom HTTP header and its value, separated by a colon ':'.

arguments.name: Sleep Requests
arguments.description: Number of seconds to sleep between requests during data extraction.

arguments.name: Sleep Interval
arguments.description: Number of seconds to sleep before each download. This is the minimum when used with --max-sleep-interval.

arguments.name: Max Sleep Interval
arguments.description: Maximum number of seconds to sleep. Can only be used along with --sleep-interval.

arguments.name: Sleep Subtitles
arguments.description: Number of seconds to sleep before each subtitle download.

arguments.name: Encoding
arguments.description: Force the specified encoding (experimental).

arguments.name: Referer
arguments.description: Specify a custom HTTP Referer header. Useful when sites block direct downloads (403 errors).

arguments.name: SponsorBlock Mark
arguments.description: SponsorBlock categories to create chapters for, separated by commas. Prefix with '-' to exclude.

arguments.name: SponsorBlock Remove
arguments.description: SponsorBlock categories to remove from the video file, separated by commas. Remove takes precedence over mark.

arguments.name: No SponsorBlock
arguments.description: Disable both --sponsorblock-mark and --sponsorblock-remove.

arguments.name: SponsorBlock API
arguments.description: SponsorBlock API location.

arguments.name: Extractor Retries
arguments.description: Number of retries for known extractor errors, or 'infinite'.

arguments.name: HLS Split Discontinuity
arguments.description: Split HLS playlists to different formats at discontinuities such as ad breaks.

arguments.name: Extractor Args
arguments.description: Arguments for the extractor. Format: KEY:ARGS. Can be specified multiple times for different extractors.

arguments.name: Write Link
arguments.description: Write an internet shortcut file (.url, .webloc, or .desktop depending on platform).

arguments.name: Write URL Link
arguments.description: Write a .url Windows internet shortcut.

arguments.name: Write Webloc Link
arguments.description: Write a .webloc macOS internet shortcut.

arguments.name: Write Desktop Link
arguments.description: Write a .desktop Linux internet shortcut.

arguments.name: URL
arguments.description: Video or playlist URL to download. Multiple URLs can be passed as separate arguments.
